content:
You already made strong points about perseverance and sacrifice, but I think these smaller symbols add even more depth. Do you think the obstacles on her journey help reinforce your argument about her endurance?

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 11, 2025 10:23AM
Raniyah Lockley
I really like how you connected Phoenix’s name to the mythological bird and how her grandson is described as a little bird too. It makes her journey feel even more meaningful like she’s doing everything she can to keep him alive. The idea of rebirth also fits because this isn’t a one-time trip. She’s walked this path so many times that it’s literally worn down, which shows her determination isn’t just a single act but something she keeps doing over and over.

Another thing that stood out to me is how her journey isn’t just about her grandson, it also reflects the struggles she faced as an elderly Black woman in the South. When the white hunter points a gun at her and asks if she’s scared, she barely reacts: “Phoenix only gave a twitch to her face as if a fly were bothering her” (Welty, 1941). That moment shows how much she’s already endured. She’s not just tough because of this journey she’s been tough her whole life.
